You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: c0066b4a).

## Issue to Resolve

Remove default for allowed wildcard imports in `ktlint_official` codestyle
>Expected Behavior
> When java.util.* is included as a wildcard import, it should trigger the warning for no-wildcard-imports.
> ...
> Given that, I think it makes sense that ktlint should stop including those two paths as default wildcard exceptions.

_Originally posted by @greg-patterson in https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1792#issuecomment-1407098815_

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

The feature to respect setting ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand  was implemented not too long ago in ktlint 0.45.x via https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1272. The reason for adding the default values is that they seems to be hard-coded in IntelliJ IDEA as well (https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82).

If the property is not set explicitly, IntelliJ IDEA allows imports java.util.* and kotlinx.android.synthetic.*. If ktlint would not do the same, this would result in a conclict between ktlint and IntelliJ IDEA. Lots of users of ktlint dislike that.

In the new codestyle `ktlint_official` the default value for allowed wildcard imports shoulbe be cleared.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `.editorconfig`
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `docs/rules/standard.md`
- `ktlint-cli/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/cli/internal/KtlintCommandLine.kt`
- `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/RuleExecutionContext.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/NoWildcardImportsRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.importordering.PatternEntry
  9→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 10→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 11→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtImportDirective
 12→
 13→public class NoWildcardImportsRule :
 14→    StandardRule(
 15→        id = "no-wildcard-imports",
 16→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 17→            IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND,
 18→        ),
 19→    ) {
 20→    private lateinit var allowedWildcardImports: List<PatternEntry>
 21→
 22→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 23→        allowedWildcardImports = editorConfig[IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND]
 24→    }
 25→
 26→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 27→        node: ASTNode,
 28→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 29→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 30→    ) {
 31→        if (node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
 32→            val importDirective = node.psi as KtImportDirective
 33→            val path = importDirective.importPath ?: return
 34→            if (!path.isAllUnder) return
 35→            if (allowedWildcardImports.none { it.matches(path) }) {
 36→                emit(node.startOffset, "Wildcard import", false)
 37→            }
 38→        }
 39→    }
 40→
 41→    public companion object {
 42→        private const val WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES = "*"
 43→        private const val WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES = "**"
 44→
 45→        private fun parseAllowedWildcardImports(allowedWildcardImports: String): List<PatternEntry> {
 46→            val importsList = allowedWildcardImports.split(",").onEach { it.trim() }
 47→
 48→            return importsList.map { import ->
 49→                if (import.endsWith(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES)) { // java.**
 50→                    PatternEntry(
 51→                        packageName = import.removeSuffix(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES).plus(WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES),
 52→                        withSubpackages = true,
 53→                        hasAlias = false,
 54→                    )
 55→                } else {
 56→                    PatternEntry(
 57→                        packageName = import,
 58→                        withSubpackages = false,
 59→                        hasAlias = false,
 60→                    )
 61→                }
 62→            }
 63→        }
 64→
 65→        private val PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER: (String, String?) -> PropertyType.PropertyValue<List<PatternEntry>> =
 66→            { _, value ->
 67→                when {
 68→                    else -> try {
 69→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
 70→                            value,
 71→                            value?.let(Companion::parseAllowedWildcardImports) ?: emptyList(),
 72→                        )
 73→                    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
 74→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
 75→                            value,
 76→                            "Unexpected imports layout: $value",
 77→                        )
 78→                    }
 79→                }
 80→            }
 81→
 82→        public val IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
 83→            EditorConfigProperty(
 84→                type = PropertyType(
 85→                    "ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand",
 86→                    "Defines allowed wildcard imports",
 87→                    PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER,
 88→                ),
 89→                /**
 90→                 * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Use wildcard imports for packages in "java.util", "kotlin.android.synthetic" and
 91→                 * it's subpackages.
 92→                 *
 93→                 * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82
 94→                 */
 95→                defaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports("java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**"),
 96→                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
 97→            )
 98→    }
 99→}
100→
101→public val NO_WILDCARD_IMPORTS_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoWildcardImportsRule().ruleId
102→

./CHANGELOG.md:41:The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
--
./CHANGELOG.md:43:The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
--
./CHANGELOG.md:227:* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-first-line-in-class-body` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule disallows a class to start with a blank line. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
./CHANGELOG.md:228:* Add new experimental rule `if-else-bracing` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rules enforces consistent usage of braces in all branches of a singe if or if-else-if statement. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
./CHANGELOG.md:229:* Add new experimental rule `no-consecutive-comments` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule disallows consecutive comments except EOL comments. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
./CHANGELOG.md:230:* Add new experimental rule `try-catch-finally-spacing` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule enforces consistent spacing in try-catch, try-finally and try-catch-finally statement. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
--

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
 2→
 3→import org.ec4j.core.model.Property
 4→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 5→
 6→/**
 7→ * Definition of '.editorconfig' property enriched with fields required by the [com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine].
 8→ *
 9→ * Properties can be:
10→ * - universal `.editorconfig` properties defined
11→ *   [here](https://github.com/editorconfig/editorconfig/wiki/EditorConfig-Properties#current-universal-properties)
12→ * - universal IntelliJ IDEA properties defined
13→ *   [here](https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-community/blob/master/platform/lang-api/src/com/intellij/psi/codeStyle/CommonCodeStyleSettings.java)
14→ * - Kotlin specific properties defined
15→ *   [here](https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/master/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java)
16→ */
17→public data class EditorConfigProperty<T>(
18→    /**
19→     * Type of property. Could be one of default ones (see [PropertyType.STANDARD_TYPES]) or custom one.
20→     */
21→    public val type: PropertyType<T>,
22→
23→    /**
24→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties. This default applies to all code styles
25→     * unless the code style specific default value is set.
26→     */
27→    public val defaultValue: T,
28→
29→    /**
30→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'ktlint_official'. When not
31→     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
32→     */
33→    public val ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
34→
35→    /**
36→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'intellij_idea'. When not
37→     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
38→     */
39→    public val intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
40→
41→    /**
42→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'android_studio'. When not
43→     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
44→     */
45→    public val androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
46→
47→    /**
48→     * If set, it maps the actual value set for the property, to another valid value for that property. See example
49→     * below where
50→     * ```kotlin
51→     * propertyMapper = { property, isAndroidCodeStyle ->
52→     *     when {
53→     *         property == null ->
54→     *             // property is not defined in ".editorconfig" file
55→     *         property.isUnset ->
56→     *             // property is defined in ".editorconfig" file with special value "unset"
57→     *         property.sourceValue == "some-string-value" ->
58→     *             // property is defined in ".editorconfig" file with a value that needs to be remapped to another
59→     *             // valid value. For example the "max_line_length" property accepts value "off" but is remapped to
60→     *             // "-1" in ktlint.
61→     *        else ->
62→     *             property.getValueAs() // or null
63→     *     }
64→     * }
65→     * ```
66→     * In case the lambda returns a null value then, the code style specific default value or the generic [defaultValue]
67→     * is used.
68→     */
69→    public val propertyMapper: ((Property?, CodeStyleValue) -> T?)? = null,
70→
71→    /**
72→     * Custom function that represents [T] as String. Defaults to the standard `toString()` call. Override the
73→     * default implementation in case you need a different behavior than the standard `toString()` (e.g. for
74→     * collections joinToString() is more applicable).
75→     */
76→    public val propertyWriter: (T) -> String = { it.toString() },
77→
78→    /**
79→     * Optional message to be displayed whenever the value of the property is being retrieved while it has been deprecated.
80→     */
81→    val deprecationWarning: String? = null,
82→
83→    /**
84→     * Optional message to be displayed whenever the value of the property is being retrieved while it has been deprecated.
85→     */
86→    val deprecationError: String? = null,
87→
88→    /**
89→     * Name of the property. A property must be named in case multiple properties are defined for the same type.
90→     * Defaults to the name of the type when not set.
91→     */
92→    public val name: String = type.name,
93→)

conversation_history.content:
1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
  5→import mu.KotlinLogging
  6→import org.ec4j.core.model.Property
  7→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
  8→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValue
  9→
 10→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 11→
 12→/**
 13→ * Loaded [Property]s from `.editorconfig` files.
 14→ */
 15→public data class EditorConfig(
 16→    private val properties: Map<String, Property> = emptyMap(),
 17→) {
 18→    public constructor(vararg properties: Property) : this(
 19→        properties.associateBy { property -> property.name },
 20→    )
 21→
 22→    private val codeStyle: CodeStyleValue
 23→        get() =
 24→            with(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY) {
 25→                // Prevent printing a lot of warnings when the code_style_value is not defined explicitly by not using the function to get
 26→                // the default value for the property
 27→                type
 28→                    .getPropertyValue(type.name)
 29→                    .parsed
 30→                    ?: defaultValue
 31→            }
 32→
 33→    /**
 34→     * Gets the value of [editorConfigProperty] from [EditorConfig] provided that the name of the property is identical to the name of the
 35→     * type of the property. If the value is not found, the default value for the matching code style is returned.
 36→     */
 37→    public operator fun <T> get(editorConfigProperty: EditorConfigProperty<T>): T {
 38→        when {
 39→            editorConfigProperty.deprecationError != null ->
 40→                throw DeprecatedEditorConfigPropertyException(
 41→                    "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is disallowed: ${editorConfigProperty.deprecationError}",
 42→                )
 43→
 44→            editorConfigProperty.deprecationWarning != null ->
 45→                LOGGER.warn { "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is deprecated: ${editorConfigProperty.deprecationWarning}" }
 46→        }
 47→        val property = properties.getOrElse(editorConfigProperty.name) {
 48→            throw IllegalStateException(
 49→                "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' can not be retrieved from this EditorConfig. Note that the EditorConfig which " +
 50→                    "is provided to class '${Rule::class.qualifiedName}' only contains the properties which are defined in the property " +
 51→                    "'${Rule::usesEditorConfigProperties.name}'.",
 52→            )
 53→        }
 54→
 55→        editorConfigProperty
 56→            .propertyMapper
 57→            ?.invoke(property, codeStyle)
 58→            ?.let { newValue ->
 59→                // If the property value is remapped to a non-null value then return it immediately.
 60→                val originalValue = property.sourceValue
 61→                if (newValue.toString() != originalValue) {
 62→                    LOGGER.trace {
 63→                        "Value of '.editorconfig' property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is remapped " +
 64→                            "from '$originalValue' to '$newValue'"
 65→                    }
 66→                }
 67→                return newValue
 68→            }
 69→
 70→        return if (property.isUnset) {
 71→            editorConfigProperty
 72→                .getDefaultValue()
 73→                .also {
 74→                    LOGGER.trace {
 75→                        "No value of '.editorconfig' property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' was found. Value has been defaulted to " +
 76→                            "'$it'. Setting the value explicitly in '.editorconfig' removes this message from the log."
 77→                    }
 78→                }
 79→        } else {
 80→            // In case the EditorConfig is loaded incorrectly it can happen that the type field of the loaded property is null. Getting the
 81→            // value via "property.getValueAs()" can lead to a class cast exception as the actual type of the value returned is transformed
 82→            // to a String instead of type T. Getting the value via the editorConfigProperty type is safe as it can not be null.
 83→            editorConfigProperty.getParsedValueOrDefault() ?: editorConfigProperty.getDefaultValue()
 84→        }
 85→    }
 86→
 87→    /**
 88→     * Gets the value of the property with [propertyType] and name [propertyName] from [EditorConfig]. Returns null if the type is not
 89→     * found. Also, returns null when no property with the name is found.
 90→     */
 91→    public fun <T> getEditorConfigValueOrNull(
 92→        propertyType: PropertyType<T>,
 93→        propertyName: String,
 94→    ): T? = propertyType.getPropertyValue(propertyName).parsed
 95→
 96→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getDefaultValue() =
 97→        when (codeStyle) {
 98→            CodeStyleValue.android -> androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue
 99→            CodeStyleValue.android_studio -> androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue
100→            CodeStyleValue.official -> intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue
101→            CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea -> intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue
102→            CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official -> ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue
103→        }
104→
105→    /**
106→     * Checks whether a property with name [propertyName] is defined.
107→     */
108→    public fun contains(propertyName: String): Boolean = properties.contains(propertyName)
109→
110→    /**
111→     * Gets the parsed value of the property with type [PropertyType]. Returns null if the property type is not found. Can only be used for
112→     * properties for which the name of the property is identical to the name of the type.
113→     */
114→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(): T? =
115→        this.type.getParsedValueOrDefault(this.type.name, this.defaultValue)
116→
117→    /**
118→     * Gets the parsed value of the property with type [PropertyType] and name [propertyName]. Returns null if the property type is not
119→     * found. Intended to be used for properties for which the name is not identical to the name of the property type.
120→     */
121→    private fun <T> PropertyType<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(
122→        propertyName: String,
123→        defaultValue: T,
124→    ): T =
125→        getPropertyValue(propertyName)
126→            .let { propertyValue ->
127→                if (propertyValue.isValid) {
128→                    propertyValue.parsed
129→                } else {
130→                    LOGGER.warn {
131→                        "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' contains an invalid value '${propertyValue.source}'. The default value " +
132→                            "'$defaultValue' is used instead."
133→                    }
134→                    defaultValue
135→                }
136→            }
137→
138→    private fun <T> PropertyType<T>.getPropertyValue(propertyName: String): PropertyValue<T> =
139→        parse(
140→            properties[propertyName]?.sourceValue,
141→        )
142→
143→    /**
144→     * Maps all properties with given [mapper] to a collection.
145→     */
146→    public fun <T> map(mapper: (Property) -> T): Collection<T> =
147→        properties
148→            .values
149→            .map { mapper(it) }
150→
151→    /**
152→     * Adds the given [EditorConfigProperty]'s with its default value for the active code style to the [EditorConfig] if the property does
153→     * not yet exist.
154→     */
155→    public fun addPropertiesWithDefaultValueIfMissing(vararg defaultEditorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperty<*>): EditorConfig {
156→        val editorConfigProperties = defaultEditorConfigProperties.toSet()
157→        requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties)
158→        return editorConfigProperties
159→            .defaultProperties()
160→            .plus(
161→                // Overwrite default properties with values that were actually already defined in the EditorConfig
162→                properties,
163→            ).let { EditorConfig(it) }
164→    }
165→
166→    /**
167→     * Creates an [EditorConfig] containing [editorConfigProperties] only. Retains the value of the property when defined in the original
168→     * [EditorConfig]. If the property does not exist in the original [EditorConfig], it will be added and its value is set to the default
169→     * value of the active code style.
170→     */
171→    public fun filterBy(editorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>): EditorConfig {
172→        requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties)
173→        return editorConfigProperties
174→            .defaultProperties()
175→            .plus(
176→                // Overwrite default properties with values that were actually already defined in the EditorConfig
177→                properties.filterBy(editorConfigProperties.map { it.name }),
178→            ).let { EditorConfig(it) }
179→    }
180→
181→    private fun requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>) {
182→        val editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities =
183→            editorConfigProperties
184→                .groupBy { it.name }
185→                .filterValues { groupedEditorConfigProperties ->
186→                    groupedEditorConfigProperties
187→                        .distinctBy { it.hashCode() }
188→                        .count() > 1
189→                }
190→        require(editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities.isEmpty()) {
191→            editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities
192→                .map { (name, editorConfigProperties) ->
193→                    editorConfigProperties
194→                        .joinToString(
195→                            separator = "\n",
196→                            prefix = "Found multiple editorconfig properties with name '$name' but having distinct identities:\n",
197→                        ) { "  - $it" }
198→                }.joinToString(separator = "\n")
199→        }
200→    }
201→
202→    private fun Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>.defaultProperties(): Map<String, Property> {
203→        return associate { editorConfigProperty ->
204→            editorConfigProperty
205→                .writeDefaultValue()
206→                .let { editorConfigProperty.name to editorConfigProperty.toPropertyWithValue(it) }
207→        }
208→    }
209→
210→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.writeDefaultValue() = propertyWriter(getDefaultValue())
211→
212→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.toPropertyWithValue(value: String): Property =
213→        Property
214→            .builder()
215→            .type(type)
216→            .name(name)
217→            .value(value)
218→            .build()
219→
220→    private fun Map<String, Property>.filterBy(editorConfigProperties: List<String>): Map<String, Property> =
221→        filterKeys { it in editorConfigProperties }
222→}
223→
224→private class DeprecatedEditorConfigPropertyException(message: String) : RuntimeException(message)
225→

237→    }
238→
239→    @Test
240→    fun `Given an editorconfig containing a property with undefined type then retrieving that property via the EditConfig may not result in an exception`() {
241→        // The Property and PropertyValue builders of the ec4j library do not allow to build a property having field "Property.type" set to
242→        // null. However, when loading an ".editorconfig" file containing a property with a name for which no property type exists with that
243→        // same name, this does result in such a property. It takes a detour to create the property:
244→        //  - Create an ".editorconfig" file on the file system mock
245→        //  - Load the ".editorconfig" via the EditorConfigDefaults loader
246→        //  - Find the property which is set in the ".editorconfig" file and validate that its type is null
247→        //  - Transform the property to a KtLint EditorConfig
248→        //  - Extract the property of the KtLint EditorConfig
249→        // Note that two properties are created which are identical except that only one of the properties has a null type and the other
250→        // property has a correctly defined type.
251→        //language=
252→        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1 = "ktlint_test_rule-1"
253→        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType1 = editorConfigProperty(ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1)
254→        //language=
255→        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2 = "ktlint_test_rule-2"
256→        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType2 = editorConfigProperty(ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2)
257→
258→        val ktlintTestFileSystem = KtlintTestFileSystem().apply {
259→            writeRootEditorConfigFile(
260→                //language=EditorConfig
261→                """
262→                [*.{kt,kts}]
263→                $ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1 = disabled
264→                $ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2 = disabled
265→                """.trimIndent(),
266→            )
267→        }
268→
269→        val ktlintTestRuleProperties =
270→            EditorConfigDefaults
271→                .load(
272→                    path = ktlintTestFileSystem.resolve(""),
273→                    propertyTypes = setOf(
274→                        // Note that ktlintTestRuleEditorConfigPropertyType1 has been left out on purpose
275→                        ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType2.type,
276→                    ),
277→                ).value
278→                .sections
279→                .flatMap { it.properties.values }
280→                .associateBy { it.name }
281→
282→        // Validate that both properties are loaded
283→        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1] != null) {
284→            "Can not find property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1' in the '.editorconfig' file on the file system mock"
285→        }
286→        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2] != null) {
287→            "Can not find property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2' in the '.editorconfig' file on the file system mock"
288→        }
289→
290→        // Validate that the type of one of the properties is null
291→        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1]?.type == null) {
292→            "Property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1' should have an undefined type"
293→        }
294→        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2]?.type != null) {
295→            "Property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2' should have a defined type"
296→        }
297→
298→        val editorConfig = EditorConfig(ktlintTestRuleProperties)
299→
300→        // Although the type of one of the properties is null, both can be loaded as the type of EditorConfigProperty is used to parse the
301→        // raw value of the property
302→        assertThat(editorConfig[ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType1]).isEqualTo(RuleExecution.disabled)
303→        assertThat(editorConfig[ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType2]).isEqualTo(RuleExecution.disabled)
304→    }
305→
306→    private fun editorConfigProperty(name: String) =
307→        EditorConfigProperty(
308→            name = name,
309→            type = PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
310→                name,
311→                "",
312→                SafeEnumValueParser(RuleExecution::class.java),
313→                RuleExecution.values().map { it.name }.toSet(),
314→            ),
315→            defaultValue = RuleExecution.enabled,
316→        )
317→
318→    private companion object {
319→        const val SOME_PROPERTY_NAME = "some-property-name"
320→        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE = "some-property-value"
321→        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_ANDROID = "some-property-value-android"
322→        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_DEFAULT = "some-property-value-default"
323→        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_INTELLIJ_IDEA = "some-property-value-intellij-idea"
324→        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_OFFICIAL = "some-property-value-official"
325→        val SOME_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY = EditorConfigProperty(
326→            name = SOME_PROPERTY_NAME,
327→            type = PropertyType(
328→                SOME_PROPERTY_NAME,
329→                "",
330→                PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.IDENTITY_VALUE_PARSER,
331→                setOf(SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_ANDROID, SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_INTELLIJ_IDEA),
332→            ),
333→            defaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_DEFAULT,
334→            androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_ANDROID,
335→            ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_OFFICIAL,
336→            intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_INTELLIJ_IDEA,
337→        )
338→    }

**Planning property default handling**

I’m focusing on ensuring that adding default properties doesn’t create unwanted empty values, especially for the official style which should have an empty list as the default. I’ll search for how properties like `PROPERTY_PARSER` and methods like `addPropertiesWithDefaultValueIfMissing` are used to understand if and when properties with default empty values get written into EditorConfig files, so the logic handles IntelliJ, Android, and official styles correctly.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigDefaults
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigDefaults.Companion.EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_DEFAULTS
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigOverride
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigOverride.Companion.EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.END_OF_LINE_PROPERTY
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.ThreadSafeEditorConfigCache.Companion.THREAD_SAFE_EDITOR_CONFIG_CACHE
 17→import mu.KotlinLogging
 18→import org.ec4j.core.EditorConfigLoader
 19→import org.ec4j.core.PropertyTypeRegistry
 20→import org.ec4j.core.Resource
 21→import org.ec4j.core.ResourcePropertiesService
 22→import org.ec4j.core.model.Property
 23→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 24→import org.ec4j.core.model.Version
 25→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.utils.addToStdlib.applyIf
 26→import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets
 27→import java.nio.file.FileSystem
 28→import java.nio.file.FileSystems
 29→import java.nio.file.Path
 30→
 31→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 32→
 33→/**
 34→ * Loader for `.editorconfig` properties for files on [fileSystem].
 35→ */
 36→internal class EditorConfigLoader(
 37→    private val fileSystem: FileSystem = FileSystems.getDefault(),
 38→    private val editorConfigLoaderEc4j: EditorConfigLoaderEc4j,
 39→    private val editorConfigDefaults: EditorConfigDefaults = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_DEFAULTS,
 40→    private val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
 41→) {
 42→    /**
 43→     * Loads properties used by [Rule]s from the `.editorconfig` file on given [filePath]. When [filePath] is null, the
 44→     * properties for the ".kt" pattern in the current directory are loaded. The '.editorconfig' files on the [filePath]
 45→     * are read starting from the [filePath] upwards until an '.editorconfig' file is found in which the property "root"
 46→     * is found with value "true" or until the root of the filesystem is reached.
 47→     *
 48→     * Properties specified in [editorConfigDefaults] will be used in case the property was not found in any
 49→     * '.editorconfig' on [filePath]. If the property is not specified in [editorConfigDefaults] then the default value
 50→     * as specified in the property definition [EditorConfigProperty] is used.
 51→     *
 52→     * Properties specified in [editorConfigOverride] take precedence above any other '.editorconfig' file on [filePath]
 53→     * or default value.
 54→     */
 55→    internal fun load(filePath: Path?): EditorConfig {
 56→        val editorConfigPath = filePath ?: defaultFilePath()
 57→        val editorConfigProperties: MutableMap<String, Property> =
 58→            createResourcePropertiesService(editorConfigLoaderEc4j.editorConfigLoader, editorConfigDefaults)
 59→                .queryProperties(editorConfigPath.resource())
 60→                .properties
 61→        return editorConfigProperties
 62→            .also { properties ->
 63→                if (properties[TAB_WIDTH_PROPERTY_NAME]?.sourceValue == properties[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY.name]?.sourceValue &&
 64→                    editorConfigOverride.properties[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY] != null
 65→                ) {
 66→                    // The tab_width property can not be overridden via the editorConfigOverride. So if it has been
 67→                    // set to the same value as the indent_size property then keep its value in sync with that
 68→                    // property.
 69→                    properties[TAB_WIDTH_PROPERTY_NAME] =
 70→                        Property
 71→                            .builder()
 72→                            .name(TAB_WIDTH_PROPERTY_NAME)
 73→                            .type(PropertyType.tab_width)
 74→                            .value(editorConfigOverride.properties[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY]?.source)
 75→                            .build()
 76→                }
 77→                editorConfigOverride
 78→                    .properties
 79→                    .forEach {
 80→                        properties[it.key.name] = it.key.toPropertyWithValue(it.value)
 81→                    }
 82→            }.also { properties ->
 83→                LOGGER.debug { properties.prettyPrint(filePath) }
 84→            }.let { properties ->
 85→                // Only add properties which are not related to rules but which are needed by the KtLint Rule Engine
 86→                EditorConfig(properties)
 87→                    .addPropertiesWithDefaultValueIfMissing(
 88→                        /**
 89→                         * Used in [EditorConfig] to determine the default value for a property when it is not specified in the
 90→                         * [EditorConfig].
 91→                         */
 92→                        CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 93→                        /**
 94→                         * Used by [KtLintRuleEngine] to use correct line separator when writing the formatted output.
 95→                         */
 96→                        END_OF_LINE_PROPERTY,
 97→                        /**
 98→                         * Used by [VisitorProvider] to determine whether experimental rules have to be executed.
 99→                         */
100→                        EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY,
101→                    )
102→            }
103→    }
104→
105→    private fun MutableMap<String, Property>.prettyPrint(normalizedFilePath: Path?) =
106→        map { entry -> "${entry.key}: ${entry.value.sourceValue}" }
107→            .joinToString(
108→                prefix = "Effective editorconfig properties${
109→                    if (normalizedFilePath == null) {
110→                        ""
111→                    } else {
112→                        " for file '$normalizedFilePath'"
113→                    }
114→                }:\n\t",
115→                separator = "\n\t",
116→            )
117→
118→    private fun Path?.resource() = Resource.Resources.ofPath(this, StandardCharsets.UTF_8)
119→
120→    private fun defaultFilePath() =
121→        fileSystem
122→            .getPath(".")
123→            .toAbsolutePath()
124→            .resolve(SUPPORTED_FILES.first())
125→
126→    private fun createResourcePropertiesService(
127→        editorConfigLoader: EditorConfigLoader,
128→        editorConfigDefaults: EditorConfigDefaults,
129→    ) = ResourcePropertiesService
130→        .builder()
131→        .keepUnset(true)
132→        .cache(THREAD_SAFE_EDITOR_CONFIG_CACHE)
133→        .loader(editorConfigLoader)
134→        .applyIf(editorConfigDefaults != EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_DEFAULTS) {
135→            defaultEditorConfigs(editorConfigDefaults.value)
136→        }.build()
137→
138→    companion object {
139→        /**
140→         * List of file extensions, editorconfig lookup will be performed.
141→         */
142→        internal val SUPPORTED_FILES = arrayOf(
143→            ".kt",
144→            ".kts",
145→        )
146→
147→        private const val TAB_WIDTH_PROPERTY_NAME = "tab_width"
148→    }
149→}
150→
151→/**
152→ * Wrapper around the native EditorConfigLoader of the ec4j library. Whenever Ktlints loads ".editorconfig" files via the ec4j library, it
153→ * needs to be aware of all native (ec4j) but also the custom property types defined in KtLint and the custom rules sets provided to KtLint.
154→ *
155→ * The ec4j library uses the convention that a property type has a name that is identical to the name of that property. If no
156→ * property type is registered for a property the property will be returned with a null type. When retrieving the property value it will be
157→ * returned as type "String" which results in class cast exceptions in KtLint's EditorConfig whenever the expected type of the variable is
158→ * not of type String.
159→ */
160→internal class EditorConfigLoaderEc4j(
161→    private val propertyTypes: Set<PropertyType<*>>,
162→) {
163→    val editorConfigLoader: EditorConfigLoader
164→        get() = EditorConfigLoader.of(Version.CURRENT, propertyTypeRegistry(propertyTypes))
165→
166→    private fun propertyTypeRegistry(propertyTypes: Set<PropertyType<*>>) =
167→        PropertyTypeRegistry
168→            .builder()
169→            .defaults()
170→            .apply {
171→                propertyTypes.forEach { type(it) }
172→            }.build()
173→}
174→

 94→                import kotlinx.android.synthetic.main.layout_name.*
 95→                """.trimIndent()
 96→            noWildcardImportsRuleAssertThat(code)
 97→                .withEditorConfigOverride(IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND to "")
 98→                .hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(2, 1, "Wildcard import")
 99→        }
100→    }
101→}
102→

140→}
141→```
142→
143→```ini
144→[*.{kt,kts}]
145→ktlint_ignore_back_ticked_identifier = false
146→```
147→
148→This setting only takes effect when rule `max-line-length` is enabled.
149→
150→## Import layouts
151→
152→By default, the same imports are allowed as in IntelliJ IDEA. The import path can be a full path, e.g. "java.util.List.*" as well as wildcard path, e.g. "kotlin.**".
153→
154→The layout can be composed by the following symbols:
155→
156→*  `*` - wildcard. There must be at least one entry of a single wildcard to match all other imports. Matches anything after a specified symbol/import as well.
157→* `|` - blank line. Supports only single blank lines between imports. No blank line is allowed in the beginning or end of the layout.
158→* `^` - alias import, e.g. "^android.*" will match all android alias imports, "^" will match all other alias imports.
159→
160→Examples:
161→```kotlin
162→ij_kotlin_imports_layout=* # alphabetical with capital letters before lower case letters (e.g. Z before a), no blank lines
163→ij_kotlin_imports_layout=*,java.**,javax.**,kotlin.**,^ # default IntelliJ IDEA style, same as alphabetical, but with "java", "javax", "kotlin" and alias imports in the end of the imports list
164→ij_kotlin_imports_layout=android.**,|,^org.junit.**,kotlin.io.Closeable.*,|,*,^ # custom imports layout
165→```
166→
167→Wildcard imports can be allowed for specific import paths (Comma-separated list, use "**" as wildcard for package and all subpackages). This setting overrides the no-wildcard-imports rule. This setting is best be used for allowing wildcard imports from libraries like Ktor where extension functions are used in a way that creates a lot of imports.
168→
169→```ini
170→[*.{kt,kts}]
171→ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand = java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**
172→```
173→
174→This setting only takes effect when rule `no-wildcard-imports` is enabled.
175→
176→## Indent size & style
177→
178→By default, indenting is done with 4 spaces per indent level in `official` Kotlin code style while a single tab is used by default in the `android` code style.
179→
180→```ini
181→[*.{kt,kts}]
182→indent_size = 4 # possible values: number (e.g. 2), "unset" (makes ktlint ignore indentation completely)  
183→indent_style = space # or "tab"
184→```
185→
186→Those settings are used by multiple rules of which rule `indent` is the most important.
187→
188→## Max line length
189→
190→By default, the maximum line length is not set. The `android` code style sets the max line length to 100 (per Android Kotlin Style Guide).
191→
192→```ini
193→[*.{kt,kts}]
194→max_line_length = off # Use "off" to ignore max line length or a positive number to set max line length
195→```
196→
197→This setting is used by multiple rules of which rule `max-line-length` is the most important.
198→
199→## Trailing comma on call site
200→
201→KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site` to *enforce* the usage of the trailing comma at call site when enabled. IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to *allow* the use of trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
202→
203→!!! note
204→    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on call site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
205→
206→    Although the [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing comma's on the call site, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
207→    
208→     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner – as all the focus is on the changed value.
209→     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements – there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
210→     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
211→
212→Example:
213→```ini
214→[*.{kt,kts}]
215→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = false
216→```
217→
218→This setting only takes effect when rule `trailing-comma-on-call-site` is enabled.
219→
220→## Trailing comma on declaration site
221→
222→KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma` to *enforce* the usage of the trailing comma at declaration site when enabled. IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to *allow* the use of trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
223→
224→!!! note
225→    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on declaration site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
226→
227→    The [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) encourages the usage of trailing comma's on the declaration site, but leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing comma's on the call site. But next to this, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
228→    
229→     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner – as all the focus is on the changed value.
230→     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements – there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
231→     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
232→
233→Example:
234→```ini
235→[*.{kt,kts}]
236→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = false # Only used for declaration site 
237→```
238→
239→This setting only takes effect when rule `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` is enabled.
240→
241→## Overriding Editorconfig properties for specific directories
242→
243→You can [override](https://editorconfig.org/#file-format-details) properties for specific directories inside your project:
244→```ini
245→[*.{kt,kts}]
246→ktlint_standard_import-ordering = disabled
247→
248→[api/*.{kt,kts}]
249→ktlint_standard_indent = disabled
250→```
251→
252→Note that the `import-ordering` rule is disabled for *all* packages including the `api` sub package. Next to this the `indent` rule is disabled for the `api` package and its sub packages.
253→

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
120→
121→## No trailing whitespaces
122→
123→Rule id: `no-trailing-spaces`
124→
125→## No `Unit` as return type 
126→
127→The `Unit` type is not allowed as return type of a function.
128→returns (`fun fn {}` instead of `fun fn: Unit {}`)
129→
130→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
131→
132→    ```kotlin
133→    fun fn() {}
134→    ```
135→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
136→
137→    ```kotlin
138→    fun fn(): Unit {}
139→    ```
140→
141→Rule id: `no-unit-return`
142→
143→## No unused imports
144→
145→Rule id: `no-unused-imports`
146→
147→## No wildcard imports
148→
149→No wildcard imports except imports listed in `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand`.
150→
151→!!! warning
152→
153→    In case property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand` is not explicitly set, it allows wildcards imports like `java.util.*` by default to keep in sync with IntelliJ IDEA behavior. To disallow *all* wildcard imports, add property below to your `.editorconfig`:
154→    ```editorconfig
155→    [*.{kt,kts}]
156→    ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand = nothing
157→    ```
158→
159→Rule id: `no-wildcard-imports`
160→
161→## Package name
162→
163→Validates that the package name matches the regular expression `[a-z][a-zA-Z\d]*(\.[a-z][a-zA-Z\d]*)*`.
164→
165→Rule id: `package-name`
166→
167→## Parameter list wrapping
168→
169→When class/function signature doesn't fit on a single line, each parameter must be on a separate line
170→
171→Rule id: `parameter-list-wrapping`
172→
173→## Parameter wrapping
174→
175→When a function or class parameter doesn't fit on a single line, wrap the type or value to a separate line
176→
177→Rule id: `parameter-wrapping`
178→
179→## Property wrapping
180→
181→When a property doesn't fit on a single line, wrap the type or value to a separate line
182→
183→Rule id: `property-wrapping`
184→
185→## String template
186→
187→Consistent string templates (`$v` instead of `${v}`, `${p.v}` instead of `${p.v.toString()}`)
188→
189→Rule id: `string-template`
190→
191→## Trailing comma on call site
192→
193→Consistent removal (default) or adding of trailing comma's on call site.
194→
195→!!! important
196→    KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site` to configure the rule. When this property is enabled, KtLint *enforces* the usage of the trailing comma at call site while IntelliJ IDEA default formatter only *allows* to use the trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
197→
198→!!! note
199→    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on call site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.

conversation_history.content:
150→!!! note
151→    All rules from the `standard` and custom rule sets are *enabled* by default and can optionally be disabled in the `.editorconfig`. All rules from the `experimental` rule set are *disabled* by default and can optionally be enabled in the `.editorconfig`.
152→
153→An individual property can be enabled or disabled with a rule property. The name of the rule property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id followed by a `_` and the rule id. Examples:
154→```editorconfig
155→ktlint_standard_final-newline = disabled # Disables the `final-newline` rule in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
156→ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = enabled # Enables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
157→ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
158→```
159→
160→!!! note
161→    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.  
162→
163→## Why is wildcard import `java.util.*` not reported by the `no-wildcard-imports` rule?
164→
165→The `no-wildcard-imports` rule forbids wildcard imports, except for imports defined in `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand`. If ths property is not set explicitly set, it allows wildcards imports like `java.util.*` by default to keep in sync with IntelliJ IDEA behavior.
166→

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
  8→
  9→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 10→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 11→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 12→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 13→
 14→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 15→
 16→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 17→
 18→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 19→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 20→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 21→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 22→
 23→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 24→
 25→#### Experimental rules
 26→
 27→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 28→
 29→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 30→
 31→#### EditorConfig
 32→
 33→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 34→
 35→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 36→
 37→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 38→
 39→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 40→
 41→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 42→
 43→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 44→
 45→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 46→
 47→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 48→
 49→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 50→
 51→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
 52→

150→
151→#### Custom Ruleset Provider `RuleSetProviderV2`
152→
153→Custom Rule Sets build for older versions of KtLint no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` interface has been replaced with `RuleSetProviderV3`. The accompanying interfaces `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` have been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` respectively.
154→
155→Contrary to `RuleSetProviderV2`, the `RuleSetProviderV3` no longer contains information about the rule set. About information now has to be specified in the new `Rule` class. This allows custom rule set providers to combine rules originating from different rule sets into a new rule set without loosing information about its origin. The typ of the id of the rule set is changed from `String` to `RuleSetId`. 
156→
157→Note that due to renaming and relocation of the `RuleSetProviderV3` interface the name of the service provider in the custom reporter needs to be changed from `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` to `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.ruleset.core.api.RuleSetProviderV3`.
158→
159→The rule id's in `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` have been changed from type `String` to `RuleId`. A `RuleId` has a value that must adhere the convention "`rule-set-id`:`rule-id`". The rule set id `standard` is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Rules created by custom rule set providers and API Consumers should use a prefix other than `standard` to mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.
160→
161→When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the `ruleId` and `about` fields in the wrapped rule, so that it is clear to users whenever they use the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint project.
162→
163→The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. As a result of those changes, the `beforeFirstNode` function in each rule has to changed to something like below: 
164→
165→```kotlin
166→public class SomeRule : Rule(
167→  ruleId = RuleId("some-rule-set:some-rule"),
168→  usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY),
169→) {
170→  private lateinit var myEditorConfigProperty: MyEditorConfigProperty
171→
172→  override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
173→    myEditorConfigProperty = editorConfig[MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY]
174→  }
175→  
176→  ...
177→}
178→```
179→
180→Fields `loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded` and `runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled` have been removed from class `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule` and are replaced with a single field `mode`. The `mode` either contains value `REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED` or `ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED`.
181→
182→#### Custom Reporter Provider `ReporterProvider`
183→
184→Custom Reporters build for older versions of KtLint are no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ReporterProvider` interface has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterProviderV2`. The accompanying interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Reporter` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterV2`.
185→
186→Note that due to renaming and relocation of the `ReporterProviderV2` interface the name of the service provider in the custom reporter needs to be changed from `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ReporterProvider` to `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterProviderV2`.
187→
188→The biggest change in the `ReporterV2` is the replacement of the `LintError` class with `KtlintCliError` class. The latter class now contains a status field which more clearly explains the difference between a lint error which can be autocorrected versus a lint error that actually has been autocorrected.
189→
190→#### Custom rules provided by API Consumer
191→
192→API Consumers provide a set of rules directly to the Ktlint Rule Engine. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule`.
193→
194→The type of the rule id's has been changed from type `String` to `RuleId`. A `RuleId` has a value that must adhere the convention "`rule-set-id`:`rule-id`". Rule set id `standard` is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Custom rules created by the API Consumer should use a prefix other than `standard` to clearly mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.
195→
196→Also, the field `About` has been added. This field specifies the name of the maintainer, and the repository url and issue tracker url of the rule.
197→
198→When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the `ruleId` and `about` fields in the wrapped rule, so that it is clear to users whenever they use the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint project.
199→
200→The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. 760→- Move disallowing blank lines in chained method calls from `no-consecutive-blank-lines` to new rule (`no-blank-lines-in-chained-method-calls`) ([#1248](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1248))

763→- Respect git hooksPath setting ([#1465](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1465))
764→- Fix formatting of a property delegate with a dot-qualified-expression `indent` ([#1340](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/ssues/1340))
765→- Keep formatting of for-loop in sync with default IntelliJ formatter (`indent`) and a newline in the expression in a for-statement should not force to wrap it `wrapping` ([#1350](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1350))
766→- Fix indentation of property getter/setter when the property has an initializer on a separate line `indent` ([#1335](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1335))

769→

The name also must comply with the Pascal Case convention. The same applies to a file containing one single top level class declaration and one ore more extension functions for that class. `filename` ([#1004](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1117))

794→## [0.45.0] - 2022-03-18
795→
796→### API Changes & RuleSet providers
797→
798→If you are not an API user nor a RuleSet provider, then you can safely skip this section. Otherwise, please read below carefully and upgrade your usage of ktlint. In this and coming releases, we are changing and adapting important parts of our API in order to increase maintainability and flexibility for future changes. Please avoid skipping a releases as that will make it harder to migrate.
799→
800→#### Retrieving ".editorconfig" property value
801→
802→This section is applicable when providing rules that depend on one or more values of ".editorconfig" properties. Property values should no longer be retrieved via *EditConfig* or directly via `userData[EDITOR_CONFIG_USER_DATA_KEY]`. Property values should now only be retrieved using method `ASTNode.getEditorConfigValue(editorConfigProperty)` of interface `UsesEditorConfigProperties` which is provided in this release. Starting from next release after the current release, the *EditConfig* and/or `userData[EDITOR_CONFIG_USER_DATA_KEY]` may be removed without further notice which will break your API or rule. To prevent disruption of your end user, you should migrate a.s.a.p.
803→
804→### Added
805→- Add experimental rule for unexpected spaces in a type reference before a function identifier (`function-type-reference-spacing`) ([#1341](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1341))
806→- Add experimental rule for incorrect spacing after a type parameter list (`type-parameter-list-spacing`) ([#1366](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1366))
807→- Add experimental rule to detect discouraged comment locations (`discouraged-comment-location`) ([#1365](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1365))
808→- Add rule to check spacing after fun keyword (`fun-keyword-spacing`) ([#1362](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1362))
809→- Add experimental rules for unnecessary spacing between modifiers in and after the last modifier in a modifier list ([#1361](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1361))
810→- New experimental rule for aligning the initial stars in a block comment when present (`experimental:block-comment-initial-star-alignment` ([#297](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/297))
811→- Respect `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand` (`no-wildcard-imports`) ([#1272](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1272))
812→- Add new experimental rules for wrapping of block comment (`comment-wrapping`) ([#1403](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1403))
813→- Add new experimental rules for wrapping of KDoc comment (`kdoc-wrapping`) ([#1403](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1403))
814→- Add experimental rule for incorrect spacing after a type parameter list (`type-parameter-list-spacing`) ([#1366](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1366))
815→- Expand check task to run tests on JDK 17 - "testOnJdk17"

818→- Fix lint message to "Unnecessary long whitespace" (`no-multi-spaces`) ([#1394](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1394))
819→- Do not remove trailing comma after a parameter of type array in an annotation (experimental:trailing-comma) ([#1379](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1379))
820→- Do not delete blank lines in KDoc (no-trailing-spaces) ([#1376](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1376))
821→- Do not indent raw string literals that are not followed by either trimIndent() or trimMargin() (`indent`) ([#1375](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1375))
822→- Revert remove unnecessary wildcard imports as introduced in Ktlint 0.43.0 (`no-unused-imports`) ([#1277](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1277)), ([#1393](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1393)), ([#1256](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1256))
823→- (Possibly) resolve memory leak ([#1216](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1216))
824→- Initialize loglevel in Main class after parsing the CLI parameters ([#1412](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1412))
825→
826→### Changed
827→- Print the rule id always in the PlainReporter ([#1121](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1121))
828→- All wrapping logic is moved from the `indent` rule to the new rule `wrapping` (as part of the `standard` ruleset). In case you currently have disabled the `indent` rule, you may want to reconsider whether this is still necessary or that you also want to disable the new `wrapping` rule to keep the status quo. Both rules can be run independent of each other. ([#835](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/835))
