151→#### Custom Ruleset Provider `RuleSetProviderV2`
152→
153→Custom Rule Sets build for older versions of KtLint no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` interface has been replaced with `RuleSetProviderV3`. The accompanying interfaces `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` have been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` respectively.
154→
155→Contrary to `RuleSetProviderV2`, the `RuleSetProviderV3` no longer contains information about the rule set. About information now has to be specified in the new `Rule` class. This allows custom rule set providers to combine rules originating from different rule sets into a new rule set without loosing information about its origin. The typ of the id of the rule set is changed from `String` to `RuleSetId`. 
156→
157→Note that due to renaming and relocation of the `RuleSetProviderV3` interface the name of the service provider in the custom reporter needs to be changed from `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` to `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.ruleset.core.api.RuleSetProviderV3`.
158→
159→The rule id's in `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` have been changed from type `String` to `RuleId`. A `RuleId` has a value that must adhere the convention "`rule-set-id`:`rule-id`". The rule set id `standard` is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Rules created by custom rule set providers and API Consumers should use a prefix other than `standard` to mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.
160→
161→When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the `ruleId` and `about` fields in the wrapped rule, so that it is clear to users whenever they use the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint project.
162→
163→The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. As a result of those changes, the `beforeFirstNode` function in each rule has to changed to something like below: 
164→
165→```kotlin
166→public class SomeRule : Rule(
167→  ruleId = RuleId("some-rule-set:some-rule"),
168→  usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY),
169→) {
170→  private lateinit var myEditorConfigProperty: MyEditorConfigProperty
171→
172→  override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
173→    myEditorConfigProperty = editorConfig[MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY]
174→  }
175→  
176→  ...
177→}
178→```
179→
180→Fields `loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded` and `runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled` have been removed from class `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule` and are replaced with a single field `mode`. The `mode` either contains value `REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED` or `ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED`.
181→
182→#### Custom Reporter Provider `ReporterProvider`
183→
184→Custom Reporters build for older versions of KtLint are no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ReporterProvider` interface has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterProviderV2`. The accompanying interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Reporter` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterV2`.
185→
186→Note that due to renaming and relocation of the `ReporterProviderV2` interface the name of the service provider in the custom reporter needs to be changed from `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ReporterProvider` to `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterProviderV2`.
187→
188→The biggest change in the `ReporterV2` is the replacement of the `LintError` class with `KtlintCliError` class. The latter class now contains a status field which more clearly explains the difference between a lint error which can be autocorrected versus a lint error that actually has been autocorrected.
189→
190→#### Custom rules provided by API Consumer
191→
192→API Consumers provide a set of rules directly to the Ktlint Rule Engine. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule`.
193→
194→The type of the rule id's has been changed from type `String` to `RuleId`. A `RuleId` has a value that must adhere the convention "`rule-set-id`:`rule-id`". Rule set id `standard` is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Custom rules created by the API Consumer should use a prefix other than `standard` to clearly mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.
195→
196→Also, the field `About` has been added. This field specifies the name of the maintainer, and the repository url and issue tracker url of the rule.
197→
198→When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the `ruleId` and `about` fields in the wrapped rule, so that it is clear to users whenever they use the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint project.
199→
200→The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. As a result of those changes, the `beforeFirstNode` function in each rule has to changed to something like below:

794→## [0.45.0] - 2022-03-18

796→### API Changes & RuleSet providers
797→
798→If you are not an API user nor a RuleSet provider, then you can safely skip this section. Otherwise, please read below carefully and upgrade your usage of ktlint. In this and coming releases, we are changing and adapting important parts of our API in order to increase maintainability and flexibility for future changes. Please avoid skipping a releases as that will make it harder to migrate.
799→
800→#### Retrieving ".editorconfig" property value
801→
802→This section is applicable when providing rules that depend on one or more values of ".editorconfig" properties. Property values should no longer be retrieved via *EditConfig* or directly via `userData[EDITOR_CONFIG_USER_DATA_KEY]`. Property values should now only be retrieved using method `ASTNode.getEditorConfigValue(editorConfigProperty)` of interface `UsesEditorConfigProperties` which is provided in this release. Starting from next release after the current release, the *EditConfig* and/or `userData[EDITOR_CONFIG_USER_DATA_KEY]` may be removed without further notice which will break your API or rule. To prevent disruption of your end user, you should migrate a.s.a.p.

221→#### `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` default value
222→
223→Previously the default value for `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` was set to `-1` in ktlint unless the property was defined explicitly in the `.editorconfig` or when `ktlint_code_style` was set to Android. As a result of that rules have to check that max_line_length contains a positive value before checking that the actual line length is exceeding the maximum. Now the value `Int.MAX_VALUE` (use constant `MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF` to refer to that value) is used instead. 
224→
225→### Added
226→
227→* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-first-line-in-class-body` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule disallows a class to start with a blank line. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
228→* Add new experimental rule `if-else-bracing` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rules enforces consistent usage of braces in all branches of a singe if or if-else-if statement. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
229→* Add new experimental rule `no-consecutive-comments` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule disallows consecutive comments except EOL comments. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
230→* Add new experimental rule `try-catch-finally-spacing` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule enforces consistent spacing in try-catch, try-finally and try-catch-finally statement. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
231→* Wrap the type or value of a function or class parameter in case the maximum line length is exceeded `parameter-wrapping` ([#1846](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1846))
232→* Wrap the type or value of a property in case the maximum line length is exceeded `property-wrapping` ([#1846](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1846))

265→* Revert to matrix build to speed up build, especially for the Windows related build ([#1787](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1787))
266→
267→## [0.48.2] - 2023-01-21
268→
269→### Additional clarification on API Changes in `0.48.0` and `0.48.1`
270→
271→Starting with Ktlint `0.48.x`, rule and rule sets can be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
272→
273→API Consumers that provide experimental rules to the KtLintRuleEngine, must also enable the experimental rules or instruct their users to do so in the `.editorconfig` file. From the perspective of the API Consumer it might be confusing or unnecessary to do so as the experimental rule was already provided explicitly.
274→
275→Ktlint wants to provide the user (e.g. a developer) a uniform and consistent user experience. The `.editorconfig` becomes more and more central to store configuration for Ktlint. This to ensure that all team members use the exact same configuration when running ktlint regardless whether the Ktlint CLI or an API Consumer is being used.
276→
277→The `.editorconfig` is a powerful configuration tool which can be used in very different ways. Most projects use a single `.editorconfig` file containing one common section for kotlin and kotlin scripts files. For example, the `.editorconfig` file of the Ktlint project contains following section:
278→```editorconfig
279→[*.{kt,kts}]
280→ij_kotlin_imports_layout = *
281→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = true
282→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = true
283→```
284→Other projects might contain multiple `.editorconfig` files for different parts of the project directory hierarchy. Or, use a single `.editorconfig` file containing multiple sections with different globs. Like all other configuration settings in Ktlint, the user should be able to enable and disable the experimental rules. Both for the entire set of experimental rules and for individual experimental rules.
285→
286→Ktlint allows API Consumers to set default values and override values for the `.editorconfig`. Specifying a default value means that the user does not need to define the property in the `.editorconfig` file but if the user specifies the value, it will take precedence. Specifying the override value ensures that this takes precedence on a value specified by the user in the `.editorconfig`.
287→
288→From the Ktlint perspective, it is advised that API Consumers provide the default value. See example below, for how to specify the `editorConfigDefault` property:
289→```
290→KtLintRuleEngine(
291→    ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
292→    editorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults(
293→        EditorConfig
294→            .builder()
295→            .section(
296→                Section
297→                    .builder()
298→                    .glob(Glob("*.{kt,kts}"))
299→                    .properties(
300→                        Property
301→                            .builder()
302→                            .name("ktlint_experimental")
303→                            .value("enabled"),
304→                    ),
305→            )
306→            .build()
307→    )
308→)
309→```
310→If the user has set property `ktlint_experimental` explicitly than that value will be used. If the value is not defined, the value provided via `editorConfigDefaults` will be used.
311→
312→If you do want to ignore the value of `ktlint_experimental` as set by the user, than you can set the EditorConfigOverride property. But as said before that is discouraged as the user might not understand why the `.editorconfig` property is being ignored (provided that the value set is not equal to the value provided by the API Consumer).

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.Code
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintParseException
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine.Companion.UTF8_BOM
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleException
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter.RuleExecutionRuleFilter
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter.RunAfterRuleFilter
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.internal.rulefilter.ruleRunners
 14→import mu.KotlinLogging
 15→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 16→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.FileASTNode
 17→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiElement
 18→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiErrorElement
 19→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.idea.KotlinLanguage
 20→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtFile
 21→
 22→private val KOTLIN_PSI_FILE_FACTORY_PROVIDER = KotlinPsiFileFactoryProvider()
 23→
 24→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 25→
 26→internal class RuleExecutionContext private constructor(
 27→    val code: Code,
 28→    val rootNode: FileASTNode,
 29→    val ruleRunners: Set<RuleRunner>,
 30→    val editorConfig: EditorConfig,
 31→    val positionInTextLocator: (offset: Int) -> LineAndColumn,
 32→) {
 33→    private lateinit var suppressionLocator: SuppressionLocator
 34→
 35→    init {
 36→        rebuildSuppressionLocator()
 37→    }
 38→
 39→    fun rebuildSuppressionLocator() {
 40→        suppressionLocator = SuppressionLocatorBuilder.buildSuppressedRegionsLocator(rootNode)
 41→    }
 42→
 43→    fun executeRule(
 44→        rule: Rule,
 45→        fqRuleId: String,
 46→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 47→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 48→    ) {
 49→        try {
 50→            rule.startTraversalOfAST()
 51→            rule.beforeFirstNode(
 52→                // The rule get access to an EditConfig which is filtered by the properties which are actually registered as being used by
 53→                // the rule. In this way it can be forced that the rule actually registers the properties that it uses and the field becomes
 54→                // reliable to be used by for example the ".editorconfig" file generator.
 55→                editorConfig.filterBy(rule.usesEditorConfigProperties),
 56→            )
 57→            this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(rootNode, rule, fqRuleId, autoCorrect, emit)
 58→            rule.afterLastNode()
 59→        } catch (e: RuleExecutionException) {
 60→            throw KtLintRuleException(
 61→                e.line,
 62→                e.col,
 63→                e.rule.ruleId.value,
 64→                """
 65→                Rule '${e.rule.ruleId.value}' throws exception in file '${code.fileName}' at position (${e.line}:${e.col})
 66→                   Rule maintainer: ${e.rule.about.maintainer}
 67→                   Issue tracker  : ${e.rule.about.issueTrackerUrl}
 68→                   Repository     : ${e.rule.about.repositoryUrl}
 69→                """.trimIndent(),
 70→                e.cause,
 71→            )
 72→        }
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    private fun executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
 76→        node: ASTNode,
 77→        rule: Rule,
 78→        fqRuleId: String,
 79→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 80→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 81→    ) {
 82→        /**
 83→         * The [suppressionLocator] can be changed during each visit of node when running [KtLintRuleEngine.format]. So a new handler is to
 84→         * be built before visiting the nodes.
 85→         */
 86→        val suppressHandler = SuppressHandler(suppressionLocator, autoCorrect, emit)
 87→        if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 88→            try {
 89→                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 90→                    rule.beforeVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 91→                }
 92→                if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 93→                    node
 94→                        .getChildren(null)
 95→                        .forEach { childNode ->
 96→                            suppressHandler.handle(childNode, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 97→                                this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
 98→                                    childNode,
 99→                                    rule,
100→                                    fqRuleId,
101→                                    autoCorrect,
102→                                    emit,
103→                                )
104→                            }
105→                        }
106→                }
107→                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
108→                    rule.afterVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
109→                }
110→            } catch (e: Exception) {
111→                if (autoCorrect) {
112→                    // line/col cannot be reliably mapped as exception might originate from a node not present in the
113→                    // original AST
114→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
115→                        rule,
116→                        0,
117→                        0,
118→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
119→                        e.cause ?: e,
120→                    )
121→                } else {
122→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(node.startOffset)
123→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
124→                        rule,
125→                        line,
126→                        col,
127→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
128→                        e.cause ?: e,
129→                    )
130→                }
131→            }
132→        }
133→    }
134→
135→    companion object {
136→        internal fun createRuleExecutionContext(
137→            ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
138→            code: Code,
139→        ): RuleExecutionContext {
140→            val psiFileFactory = KOTLIN_PSI_FILE_FACTORY_PROVIDER.getKotlinPsiFileFactory(ktLintRuleEngine.isInvokedFromCli)
141→            val normalizedText = normalizeText(code.content)
142→            val positionInTextLocator = buildPositionInTextLocator(normalizedText)
143→
144→            val psiFileName =
145→                code.fileName
146→                    ?: if (code.script) {
147→                        "File.kts"
148→                    } else {
149→                        "File.kt"
150→                    }
151→            val psiFile = psiFileFactory.createFileFromText(
152→                psiFileName,
153→                KotlinLanguage.INSTANCE,
154→                normalizedText,
155→            ) as KtFile
156→
157→            psiFile
158→                .findErrorElement()
159→                ?.let { errorElement ->
160→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(errorElement.textOffset)
161→                    throw KtLintParseException(line, col, errorElement.errorDescription)
162→                }
163→
164→            val rootNode = psiFile.node
165→
166→            val editorConfig =
167→                ktLintRuleEngine
168→                    .editorConfigLoader
169→                    .load(code.filePath)
170→                    .also {
171→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
172→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("disabled_rules", "0.49")
173→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
174→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("ktlint_disabled_rules", "0.49")
175→                    }
176→
177→            val ruleRunners =
178→                ktLintRuleEngine.ruleRunners(
179→                    RuleExecutionRuleFilter(editorConfig),
180→                    RunAfterRuleFilter(),
181→                )
182→
183→            return RuleExecutionContext(
184→                code,
185→                rootNode,
186→                ruleRunners,
187→                editorConfig,
188→                positionInTextLocator,
189→            )
190→        }
191→
192→        private fun normalizeText(text: String): String {
193→            return text
194→                .replace("\r\n", "\n")
195→                .replace("\r", "\n")
196→                .replaceFirst(UTF8_BOM, "")
197→        }
198→
199→        private fun PsiElement.findErrorElement(): PsiErrorElement? {
200→            if (this is PsiErrorElement) {
201→                return this
202→            }
203→            this.children.forEach { child ->
204→                val errorElement = child.findErrorElement()
205→                if (errorElement != null) {
206→                    return errorElement
207→                }
208→            }
209→            return null
210→        }
211→    }
212→}
213→
214→private fun EditorConfig.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete(
215→    propertyName: String,
216→    ktlintVersion: String,
217→) {
218→    if (this.contains(propertyName)) {
219→        LOGGER.warn {
220→            "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' is obsolete and is not used by KtLint starting from version $ktlintVersion. Remove " +
221→                "the property from all '.editorconfig' files."
222→        }
223→    }
224→}
225→
226→private class RuleExecutionException(
227→    val rule: Rule,
228→    val line: Int,
229→    val col: Int,
230→    override val cause: Throwable,
231→) : Throwable(cause)

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.importordering
  2→
  3→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.resolve.ImportPath
  4→
  5→/**
  6→ * Represents an entry in the imports layout pattern. Contains matching logic for imports.
  7→ *
  8→ * Adopted from https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinPackageEntry.kt#L10
  9→ */
 10→public class PatternEntry(
 11→    packageName: String,
 12→    public val withSubpackages: Boolean,
 13→    public val hasAlias: Boolean,
 14→) {
 15→    private val packageName = packageName.removeSuffix(".*")
 16→
 17→    private fun matchesPackageName(otherPackageName: String): Boolean {
 18→        if (this == ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY || this == ALL_OTHER_ALIAS_IMPORTS_ENTRY) return true
 19→        if (this == BLANK_LINE_ENTRY) return false
 20→
 21→        if (otherPackageName.startsWith(packageName)) {
 22→            if (otherPackageName.length == packageName.length) {
 23→                return true
 24→            }
 25→            if (withSubpackages && otherPackageName[packageName.length] == '.') {
 26→                return true
 27→            }
 28→        }
 29→        return false
 30→    }
 31→
 32→    internal fun matches(import: ImportPath): Boolean {
 33→        return matchesPackageName(import.pathStr.removeSuffix(".*"))
 34→    }
 35→
 36→    internal fun isBetterMatchForPackageThan(
 37→        entry: PatternEntry?,
 38→        import: ImportPath,
 39→    ): Boolean {
 40→        if (hasAlias != import.hasAlias() || !matchesPackageName(import.pathStr)) return false
 41→        if (entry == null) return true
 42→
 43→        if (entry.hasAlias != hasAlias) return false
 44→        // Any matched package is better than ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY
 45→        if (this == ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY) return false
 46→        if (entry == ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY) return true
 47→
 48→        if (entry.withSubpackages != withSubpackages) return !withSubpackages
 49→
 50→        return entry.packageName.count { it == '.' } < packageName.count { it == '.' }
 51→    }
 52→
 53→    override fun toString(): String =
 54→        when (this) {
 55→            ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY -> WILDCARD_CHAR
 56→            ALL_OTHER_ALIAS_IMPORTS_ENTRY -> ALIAS_CHAR
 57→            else -> "$packageName.$WILDCARD_CHAR" + if (withSubpackages) WILDCARD_CHAR else ""
 58→        }
 59→
 60→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
 61→        if (this === other) return true
 62→        if (javaClass != other?.javaClass) return false
 63→
 64→        other as PatternEntry
 65→
 66→        if (withSubpackages != other.withSubpackages) return false
 67→        if (hasAlias != other.hasAlias) return false
 68→        if (packageName != other.packageName) return false
 69→
 70→        return true
 71→    }
 72→
 73→    override fun hashCode(): Int {
 74→        var result = withSubpackages.hashCode()
 75→        result = 31 * result + hasAlias.hashCode()
 76→        result = 31 * result + packageName.hashCode()
 77→        return result
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    internal companion object {
 81→        val BLANK_LINE_ENTRY: PatternEntry =
 82→            PatternEntry(
 83→                BLANK_LINE_CHAR,
 84→                withSubpackages = true,
 85→                hasAlias = false,
 86→            )
 87→
 88→        val ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY: PatternEntry =
 89→            PatternEntry(
 90→                WILDCARD_CHAR,
 91→                withSubpackages = true,
 92→                hasAlias = false,
 93→            )
 94→
 95→        val ALL_OTHER_ALIAS_IMPORTS_ENTRY: PatternEntry =
 96→            PatternEntry(
 97→                ALIAS_CHAR,
 98→                withSubpackages = true,
 99→                hasAlias = true,
100→            )
101→    }
102→}
